summary: CNN《今晨》报道美国250周年独立日庆祝活动，特朗普在暴风雨延误后发表40分钟演讲，强调美国历史、军事胜利并推动《拯救美国法案》。极端高温和雷暴导致东海岸多地活动取消或延迟，华盛顿特区51人因高温就医，弗吉尼亚一列车因铁轨变形脱轨，纽约一座桥梁因金属膨胀卡住。伊朗民众继续哀悼前最高领袖哈梅内伊。特朗普将赴土耳其参加北约峰会。CNN分析参议院中期选举关键席位。

vocabulary.en: to praise or promote something in a boastful or exaggerated way
vocabulary.excerpt: He touted past U.S. military victories and added some of the military operations initiated by his administration into the mix.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他吹嘘过去的美国军事胜利，并将自己政府发起的军事行动也加入其中。

vocabulary.en: to finish or complete something in a fitting or impressive way
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump took center stage to cap off the day's events.
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普总统登上舞台，为当天的活动画上句号。

vocabulary.cn: 征服荒野（美国西进运动常用表述）
vocabulary.en: to bring wild, undeveloped land under control and make it usable
vocabulary.excerpt: Americans crossed the Great Plains, scaled the Rockies, tamed the wilderness and conquered the frontier and built the empire.
vocabulary.example_cn: 美国人穿越了大平原，攀登了落基山脉，征服了荒野，开拓了边疆，建立了帝国。

vocabulary.cn: 自由帝国（美国早期扩张时期的概念，指以自由为旗帜的扩张）
vocabulary.en: a historical concept referring to the United States as an expanding nation founded on liberty
vocabulary.excerpt: It's called the empire of liberty.
vocabulary.example_cn: 它被称为自由帝国。

vocabulary.en: to completely change or improve a system, law, etc.
vocabulary.excerpt: he also argued for the passage of the Save America Act, his proposed bill that would overhaul federal elections.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他还主张通过《拯救美国法案》，这是他提出的将彻底改革联邦选举的法案。

vocabulary.en: extremely hot; very intense or harsh
vocabulary.excerpt: the celebrations faced major disruptions from blistering triple digit heat during the day to severe evening thunderstorms
vocabulary.example_cn: 庆祝活动面临重大干扰，从白天三位数的酷热到傍晚的强雷暴。

vocabulary.en: to discourage someone from doing something
vocabulary.excerpt: Delayed but not deterred, President Donald Trump still took to the stage for a late night speech.
vocabulary.example_cn: 虽然延迟但并未被阻止，唐纳德·特朗普总统仍然登台发表了深夜演讲。

vocabulary.en: the process of moving people from a dangerous place to a safe one
vocabulary.excerpt: A severe thunderstorm forced an evacuation just a couple hours later.
vocabulary.example_cn: 几小时后，一场强雷暴迫使人们疏散。

vocabulary.en: to leave the tracks accidentally
vocabulary.excerpt: Extreme heat can sometimes cause metal rails to buckle. No one was injured and all of the passengers were able to evacuate safely.
vocabulary.example_cn: 极端高温有时会导致金属轨道弯曲。无人受伤，所有乘客均安全疏散。

vocabulary.cn: 弯曲，变形（因压力或高温）
vocabulary.en: to bend or become misshapen due to pressure or heat
vocabulary.excerpt: Extreme heat can sometimes cause metal rails to buckle.
vocabulary.example_cn: 极端高温有时会导致金属轨道弯曲。

vocabulary.en: to spray water on something using a hose
vocabulary.excerpt: Fireboats were brought in to hose it down to try to cool it off and get it working again.
vocabulary.example_cn: 消防船被调来用水管冲洗桥梁，试图使其冷却并恢复正常运行。

vocabulary.en: to feel or express great sadness, especially because of someone's death
vocabulary.excerpt: Iranians are once again gathering to mourn former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
vocabulary.example_cn: 伊朗人再次聚集哀悼前最高领袖阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊。

vocabulary.en: showing a refusal to obey or comply; challenging
vocabulary.excerpt: The defiant message coming from Tehran this morning.
vocabulary.example_cn: 今早来自德黑兰的挑衅性信息。

vocabulary.en: a fierce and acrimonious struggle
vocabulary.excerpt: a bitter battle brewing for control of the Senate in the November midterms.
vocabulary.example_cn: 11月中期选举中，一场争夺参议院控制权的激烈斗争正在酝酿。

vocabulary.cn: （政治）翻转，易手（指席位或选区从一党转向另一党）
vocabulary.en: to change from one political party to another, especially in an election
vocabulary.excerpt: CNN has a look at the states that are most likely to flip and what that means for the balance of power.
vocabulary.example_cn: CNN关注了最可能易手的州以及这对权力平衡意味着什么。

vocabulary.en: a situation in which different groups or countries have equal power or influence
vocabulary.excerpt: what that means for the balance of power.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这对权力平衡意味着什么。

vocabulary.en: a situation in which an accident or disaster nearly happens
vocabulary.excerpt: Plus, a close call over the skies of Chicago.
vocabulary.example_cn: 此外，芝加哥上空发生了一次险情。

vocabulary.cn: 成为焦点，占据中心位置
vocabulary.en: to become the main focus of attention
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump took center stage to cap off the day's events.
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普总统成为焦点，为当天的活动画上句号。

vocabulary.en: for a limited period of time
vocabulary.excerpt: severe evening thunderstorms that actually temporarily evacuated the National Mall.
vocabulary.example_cn: 傍晚的强雷暴实际上暂时疏散了国家广场。

vocabulary.en: to arrange for an event to take place at a later time
vocabulary.excerpt: the opening of the Great American State Fair was postponed by two hours, Saturday.
vocabulary.example_cn: 周六，美国大州博览会的开幕式推迟了两个小时。

vocabulary.en: to lose consciousness
vocabulary.excerpt: A lot of people were passing out.
vocabulary.example_cn: 很多人昏倒了。

vocabulary.cn: 第一响应人（如警察、消防员、急救人员）
vocabulary.en: a person who is among the first to arrive at an emergency scene
vocabulary.excerpt: First responders were on hand to quickly respond to any emergencies.
vocabulary.example_cn: 第一响应人在场随时准备快速应对任何紧急情况。

vocabulary.en: present or available
vocabulary.excerpt: First responders were on hand to quickly respond to any emergencies.
vocabulary.example_cn: 第一响应人在场随时准备快速应对任何紧急情况。

vocabulary.en: the degree to which the air is clean and free from pollutants
vocabulary.excerpt: The heat is also causing unhealthy air quality in some cities.
vocabulary.example_cn: 高温还导致一些城市空气质量不健康。

sentences.en: Extreme heat and storms delayed but did not stop the July celebrations across the country for the fourth.
sentences.cn: 极端高温和暴风雨推迟了但并未阻止全国范围内为七月四日举行的庆祝活动。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语+并列谓语（delayed but did not stop），宾语+地点状语+时间状语。
sentences.analysis: but连接两个谓语动词，表示转折；'the fourth'是July 4th的简写，新闻中常用序数词指代日期。

sentences.en: In Washington after a storm related delay of nearly two hours, President Trump took center stage to cap off the day's events.
sentences.cn: 在华盛顿，经过近两小时的风暴相关延误后，特朗普总统登上中心舞台，为当天的活动画上句号。
sentences.structure: 介词短语（In Washington after...）作状语，主句主语+谓语+不定式短语作目的状语。
sentences.analysis: 'storm related delay'是复合名词，相当于'storm-related delay'；'cap off'意为'圆满结束'。

sentences.en: He touted past U.S. military victories and added some of the military operations initiated by his administration into the mix.
sentences.cn: 他吹嘘了美国过去的军事胜利，并将他政府发起的一些军事行动也加入了其中。
sentences.structure: 并列谓语（touted...and added...），'initiated by his administration'是过去分词短语作定语修饰'operations'。
sentences.analysis: 'touted'意为'吹嘘、标榜'；'into the mix'是口语化表达，意为'加入其中'。

sentences.en: Our rise to being the world's strongest and most powerful nation was no accident of history.
sentences.cn: 我们崛起成为世界上最强大、最有影响力的国家并非历史偶然。
sentences.structure: 主语（Our rise to being...）+系动词+表语（no accident of history）。
sentences.analysis: 'rise to being...'是动名词短语作主语；'no accident of history'是固定表达，意为'绝非偶然'。

sentences.en: We rebuilt our military in my first term, we use it a little bit in our, actually, I should say third term, but I won't do that because I don't want any controversy.
sentences.cn: 我们在我的第一个任期内重建了军队，我们在我们的——实际上，我应该说第三个任期——用了一点，但我不会那么说，因为我不想引起任何争议。
sentences.structure: 并列句，由but连接；第一个分句含插入语'actually, I should say third term'；第二个分句含because引导的原因状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 特朗普的口误和修正体现了口语特点；'use it a little bit'是委婉说法，指军事行动。

sentences.en: Americans crossed the Great Plains, scaled the Rockies, tamed the wilderness and conquered the frontier and built the empire.
sentences.cn: 美国人穿越了大平原，攀登了落基山脉，驯服了荒野，征服了边疆，建立了帝国。
sentences.structure: 并列谓语结构，五个动词短语并列，主语相同。
sentences.analysis: 排比修辞增强气势；'scaled'意为'攀登'；'tamed'意为'驯服'；'conquered the frontier'是历史用语。

sentences.en: While the president largely stuck to his script, he did get political in his remarks.
sentences.cn: 虽然总统基本遵循了讲稿，但他的言论确实涉及了政治。
sentences.structure: While引导让步状语从句，主句用did强调谓语动词。
sentences.analysis: 'stuck to his script'意为'按讲稿发言'；'did get political'中did表示强调，意为'确实变得政治化'。

sentences.en: He brought up communism multiple times throughout his speech, and he also argued for the passage of the Save America Act, his proposed bill that would overhaul federal elections.
sentences.cn: 他在演讲中多次提到共产主义，还主张通过《拯救美国法案》，这是他提出的将彻底改革联邦选举的法案。
sentences.structure: 并列句，第二个分句含同位语'his proposed bill'和that引导的定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 'brought up'意为'提及'；'argued for'意为'主张'；'overhaul'意为'彻底改革'。

sentences.en: So following Trump's remarks, a massive 40-minute firework display took place over D.C. skies.
sentences.cn: 因此，在特朗普讲话之后，一场长达40分钟的大型烟花表演在华盛顿特区上空举行。
sentences.structure: 现在分词短语（following...）作时间状语，主句主语+谓语+地点状语。
sentences.analysis: 'following'相当于'after'；'massive'强调规模大；'took place'是固定短语。

sentences.en: The fireworks launched from 10 different sites across Washington, D.C., and may have set a world record for the largest firework display in history.
sentences.cn: 烟花从华盛顿特区10个不同地点发射，可能创造了历史上最大烟花表演的世界纪录。
sentences.structure: 并列谓语（launched...and may have set...），第二个谓语含情态动词may have done表示推测。
sentences.analysis: 'set a world record'是固定搭配；'may have set'表示对过去事件的推测。

sentences.en: Staying with America's 250th anniversary, it was a historic milestone, years in the making, but the celebrations faced major disruptions from blistering triple digit heat during the day to severe evening thunderstorms that actually temporarily evacuated the National Mall.
sentences.cn: 继续关注美国250周年纪念，这是一个历史性的里程碑，酝酿多年，但庆祝活动面临重大干扰，从白天的酷热三位数高温到晚上的严重雷暴，后者实际上导致国家广场暂时疏散。
sentences.structure: 现在分词短语（Staying with...）作状语，主句由but连接两个并列分句；第二个分句含from...to...结构和that引导的定语从句。
sentences.analysis: 'years in the making'意为'多年筹备'；'blistering'形容极热；'triple digit heat'指华氏100度以上；'evacuated'意为'疏散'。

sentences.en: Gates opened at the Salute to America event on the National Mall around 5:00 p.m., Saturday, despite the intense heat.
sentences.cn: 尽管酷热难耐，周六下午5点左右，国家广场上的“向美国致敬”活动的大门还是打开了。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语+谓语+时间地点状语，despite介词短语作让步状语。
sentences.analysis: 'Salute to America'是活动名称；'despite'表示让步，后接名词短语。

sentences.en: A severe thunderstorm forced an evacuation just a couple hours later.
sentences.cn: 几小时后，一场严重的雷暴迫使人们疏散。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语+谓语+宾语+时间状语。
sentences.analysis: 'forced an evacuation'是常见新闻表达，意为'迫使疏散'。

sentences.en: Delayed but not deterred, President Donald Trump still took to the stage for a late night speech.
sentences.cn: 虽然被推迟但并未被吓倒，唐纳德·特朗普总统仍然登台发表了深夜演讲。
sentences.structure: 形容词短语（Delayed but not deterred）作状语，主句主语+谓语+目的状语。
sentences.analysis: 'Delayed but not deterred'是省略结构，相当于'Although he was delayed but not deterred'；'took to the stage'意为'登台'。

sentences.en: Trump's celebration for America's 250th anniversary promised a record breaking firework show.
sentences.cn: 特朗普为美国250周年纪念举办的庆祝活动承诺了一场破纪录的烟花表演。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语+谓语+宾语。
sentences.analysis: 'record breaking'是复合形容词，意为'破纪录的'。

sentences.en: Over the last two days, a fierce heat wave also forced organizers to alter or cancel events in the capital and in communities all along the East Coast.
sentences.cn: 在过去两天里，一场猛烈的热浪还迫使组织者改变或取消了首都和东海岸沿线社区的活动。
sentences.structure: 时间状语+主语+谓语+宾语+宾语补足语（to alter or cancel...）。
sentences.analysis: 'forced...to...'是常见结构；'all along the East Coast'意为'沿东海岸一线'。

sentences.en: In Washington, D.C., the opening of the Great American State Fair was postponed by two hours, Saturday.
sentences.cn: 在华盛顿特区，周六，“美国大州博览会”的开幕被推迟了两小时。
sentences.structure: 地点状语+主语+谓语+状语（by two hours）+时间状语。
sentences.analysis: 'postponed by...'表示推迟的时间长度。

sentences.en: First responders were on hand to quickly respond to any emergencies.
sentences.cn: 急救人员随时待命，以快速应对任何紧急情况。
sentences.structure: 主语+系动词+表语（on hand）+不定式短语作目的状语。
sentences.analysis: 'first responders'指急救人员；'on hand'意为'在场、随时可用'。

sentences.en: Fourth of July is typically our busiest day of the year, where we run five to 600 calls a day.
sentences.cn: 七月四日通常是我们一年中最忙的一天，我们每天处理五到六百个电话。
sentences.structure: 主句+where引导的非限制性定语从句，修饰day。
sentences.analysis: 'run calls'是口语表达，意为'处理电话'；'five to 600'可能是口误或范围。

sentences.en: On top of that, the extended fireworks displays will likely only make the air quality worse.
sentences.cn: 除此之外，延长的烟花表演很可能只会使空气质量变得更差。
sentences.structure: 介词短语（On top of that）作状语，主句主语+谓语+宾语+宾语补足语（worse）。
sentences.analysis: 'On top of that'意为'除此之外'；'make...worse'是常见结构。

topics.title: 美国250周年独立日庆祝活动
topics.content: 2026年7月4日是美国独立250周年（Sestercentennial），华盛顿特区国家广场举行“向美国致敬”活动，包括总统演讲和创纪录的烟花表演。极端高温和雷暴导致活动延迟，但未取消。特朗普在演讲中强调美国历史、军事成就，并推动《拯救美国法案》（Save America Act），要求选民出示身份证和公民身份证明，限制邮寄选票。
topics.keywords: 250th anniversary · Salute to America · Save America Act · voter ID

topics.title: 极端高温对美国基础设施的影响
topics.content: 2026年7月初，美国东海岸遭遇热浪，气温超过100°F（约38°C）。高温导致弗吉尼亚州一列客运列车因铁轨热胀弯曲（sun kink）而脱轨，纽约州布法罗市一座桥梁因金属膨胀卡在开启位置，消防船用约300万加仑水冷却桥梁。华盛顿特区51人因高温相关疾病接受治疗。
topics.keywords: heat wave · sun kink · bridge stuck · heat-related illness

topics.title: 伊朗前最高领袖哈梅内伊去世与哀悼
topics.content: 伊朗最高领袖阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊（Ayatollah Ali Khamenei）于2026年去世，伊朗民众持续举行哀悼活动。哈梅内伊自1989年起担任最高领袖，是伊朗伊斯兰共和国的最高权力象征。他的去世引发对伊朗未来政治走向和继任问题的关注。
topics.keywords: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei · Supreme Leader · mourning · Tehran

topics.title: 2026年美国中期选举参议院席位争夺
topics.content: 2026年11月美国中期选举将决定参议院控制权。CNN分析指出，多个州可能发生政党翻转（flip），影响权力平衡。特朗普政府政策、经济、社会议题将成为竞选焦点。参议院目前两党席位接近，任何一党失去席位都可能改变立法议程。
topics.keywords: midterm elections · Senate · flip · balance of power

quiz.question: 在原文 'He touted past U.S. military victories' 中，'touted' 最接近的含义是？
quiz.explanation: tout 意为‘吹捧、宣扬’，常含贬义，指过分夸赞。原文中特朗普在演讲中宣扬过去的军事胜利，符合‘吹捧’之意。

quiz.question: 短语 'cap off' 在 'President Trump took center stage to cap off the day's events' 中的意思是？
quiz.explanation: cap off 意为‘圆满结束’，指以某种方式为活动画上句号。特朗普的演讲是当天活动的压轴环节。

quiz.question: 原文 'a bitter battle brewing for control of the Senate' 中 'brewing' 的含义是？
quiz.explanation: brew 原指‘酿造’，引申为‘酝酿、即将发生’。这里指参议院控制权的激烈斗争正在酝酿之中。

quiz.question: 根据报道，特朗普在独立日演讲中主要强调了什么？
options: B. 美国历史与军事成就
quiz.explanation: 报道指出特朗普的演讲大部分聚焦于美国历史、军事胜利，并提及他政府发起的军事行动，同时推动《拯救美国法案》。

quiz.question: 极端高温对美国基础设施造成了哪些影响？
options: B. 导致列车脱轨和桥梁卡住
quiz.explanation: 报道提到弗吉尼亚州一列火车因铁轨弯曲脱轨，纽约一座桥梁因金属膨胀卡住，消防船用水冷却。

quiz.question: 关于伊朗的报道，以下哪项正确？
options: A. 伊朗人庆祝新领袖上任
B. 伊朗人哀悼前最高领袖哈梅内伊
C. 伊朗与美国关系改善
quiz.explanation: 报道明确说‘Iranians are once again gathering to mourn former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’，即哀悼前最高领袖。

paragraphs.speaker: Brad Smith
paragraphs.text: Welcome to CNN THIS MORNING. Here's what you need to know to start your Sunday morning. Extreme heat and storms delayed but did not stop the July celebrations across the country for the fourth. A look at how Americans celebrated the country's 250th anniversary and President Trump's message to the country, coming up. And happening now, Iranians are once again gathering to mourn former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The defiant message coming from Tehran this morning.
paragraphs.cn: 欢迎收看《CNN今晨》。以下是您开启周日上午所需了解的内容。极端高温和暴风雨虽造成延误，但并未阻止全国各地的七月四日庆祝活动。一起来看看美国人如何庆祝国家250周年诞辰，以及特朗普总统对全国发表的讲话，即将播出。与此同时，伊朗人再次聚集，悼念前最高领袖阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊。今早来自德黑兰的挑衅信息传来。

paragraphs.speaker: Brad Smith
paragraphs.text: And a bitter battle brewing for control of the Senate in the November midterms. And for the first time, CNN has a look at the states that are most likely to flip and what that means for the balance of power. Plus, a close call over the skies of Chicago. (BEGIN AUDIO CLIP)
paragraphs.cn: 此外，一场争夺参议院控制权的激烈战斗正在为11月中期选举酝酿。CNN首次盘点了最有可能翻盘的州，以及这对权力平衡意味着什么。另外，芝加哥上空发生惊险一幕。（音频开始）

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Pilot
paragraphs.text: Tower, we just had a firework hit our plane. Delta 1076. We're continuing.
paragraphs.cn: 塔台，我们刚刚被烟花击中了飞机。达美1076。我们继续飞行。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Air Traffic Control
paragraphs.text: Roger. (END AUDIO CLIP)
paragraphs.cn: 收到。（音频结束）

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: It is Sunday, July 5th. Thank you so much for joining us. I'm Brad Smith in for Victor Blackwell. Concerts, parades, fireworks lit up the cities in all 50 states for America's 250th birthday. In Washington after a storm related delay of nearly two hours, President Trump took center stage to cap off the day's events. The president devoted much of his speech to America's history and its strength. He touted past U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 今天是7月5日，星期日。非常感谢您的收看。我是布拉德·史密斯，代维克多·布莱克威尔主持。音乐会、游行、烟花照亮了全美50个州的城市，庆祝美国250岁生日。在华盛顿，因暴风雨延误近两小时后，特朗普总统登上中心舞台，为当天的活动画上句号。总统的演讲大部分聚焦于美国的历史和力量。他赞扬了美国过去的军事胜利，并加入了他政府发起的一些军事行动。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: military victories and added some of the military operations initiated by his administration into the mix.
paragraphs.cn: 我们崛起为世界上最强大、最有力的国家并非历史偶然。我在第一个任期内重建了我们的军队。我们在……实际上，我应该说第三个任期，但我不想引起争议，所以我不这么说。但我们使用了它，并取得了巨大成功。看看委内瑞拉，看看伊朗。我们摧毁了它。摧毁了他们的军队。美国人穿越了大平原，翻越了落基山脉，驯服了荒野，征服了边疆，建立了帝国。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: Our rise to being the world's strongest and most powerful nation was no accident of history. We rebuilt our military in my first term. We use it a little bit in our, actually, I should say third term, but I won't do that because I don't want any controversy. But we use it and we've had tremendous success. You look at Venezuela, you look at Iran. We wiped it out. Wiped out their military. Americans crossed the Great Plains, scaled the Rockies, tamed the wilderness and conquered the frontier and built the empire.
paragraphs.cn: 它被称为自由帝国。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: It's called the empire of liberty.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普还感谢了为美国自由而战的英雄和退伍军人。他请出了三位二战老兵，称他们为真正的"最伟大的一代"。虽然总统基本遵循了讲稿，但他的讲话中确实涉及了政治。他在演讲中多次提到共产主义，并主张通过《拯救美国法案》，这是他提出的旨在改革联邦选举的法案。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Trump also thanked U.S. heroes and veterans who fought for America's freedom. He brought out three veterans from World War II, calling them truly the greatest generation. While the president largely stuck to his script, he did get political in his remarks. He brought up communism multiple times throughout his speech, and he also argued for the passage of the Save America Act, his proposed bill that would overhaul federal elections.
paragraphs.cn: 今天，我们的国家再次获胜，而且我们以前所未有的方式获胜。美国回来了，我们希望保持美国的伟大，我们将通过批准《拯救美国法案》来实现这一点，这意味着所有选民必须出示选民身份证。所有选民必须提供一种叫做公民身份证明的东西。除了生病、残疾、军事部署或旅行外，将不再有邮寄选票。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.text: Today, our country is winning again, and we're winning like never before. America is back, and we want to keep America great, and we will do so by approving the Save America Act, which means all voters must show voter I.D. All voters must provide a little thing called proof of citizenship. And there will be no mail in ballots except for illness, disability, military deployment, or travel.
paragraphs.cn: 今天，我们的国家再次获胜，而且我们正在以前所未有的方式获胜。美国回来了，我们希望保持美国的伟大，我们将通过批准《拯救美国法案》来实现这一目标，这意味着所有选民必须出示选民身份证。所有选民必须提供一种叫做公民身份证明的小东西。除了生病、残疾、军事部署或旅行外，将不再有邮寄选票。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: So following Trump's remarks, a massive 40-minute firework display took place over D.C. skies. The fireworks launched from 10 different sites across Washington, D.C., and may have set a world record for the largest firework display in history. Errol Louis joins us now -- well, later on to break down the big themes in Trump's address. Really excited for that conversation, clearly. That address to the nation and the reactions from lawmakers going to be broken down in that conversation.
paragraphs.cn: 在特朗普发表讲话后，华盛顿特区上空上演了一场长达40分钟的盛大烟花表演。烟花从华盛顿特区10个不同地点发射，可能创下了历史上最大规模烟花表演的世界纪录。埃罗尔·路易斯稍后将加入我们，分析特朗普讲话中的重大主题。我们非常期待那场对话。那场对全国的讲话以及立法者的反应将在对话中详细分析。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Staying with America's 250th anniversary, it was a historic milestone, years in the making, but the celebrations faced major disruptions from blistering triple digit heat during the day to severe evening thunderstorms that actually temporarily evacuated the National Mall. Michael Yoshida is in Washington, D.C. with the details on how the weather affected yesterday's celebrations.
paragraphs.cn: 继续关注美国250周年庆典，这是一个筹备多年的历史性里程碑，但庆祝活动遭遇了重大干扰：白天酷热难耐，气温高达三位数（华氏度），晚上又有强雷暴，导致国家广场一度临时疏散。迈克尔·吉田在华盛顿特区带来天气如何影响昨天庆祝活动的详细报道。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: (BEGIN VIDEOTAPE) MICHAEL YOSHIDA, CNN NATIONAL CORRESPONDENT (voice-over): Gates opened at the Salute to America event on the National Mall around 5:00 p.m., Saturday, despite the intense heat. A severe thunderstorm forced an evacuation just a couple hours later. Delayed but not deterred, President Donald Trump still took to the stage for a late night speech.
paragraphs.cn: （开始录像）CNN国家记者迈克尔·吉田（画外音）：周六下午5点左右，尽管酷热难耐，国家广场上的"致敬美国"活动大门开启。几小时后，一场强雷暴迫使人群疏散。虽然推迟，但并未受阻，唐纳德·特朗普总统仍登上舞台发表了深夜演讲。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.text: This is the big day. We want July fourth. We're not looking for July something else, because this is the 250th anniversary of the declaration of independence. Nothing like it. YOSHIDA (voice-over): Trump's celebration for America's 250th anniversary promised a record breaking firework show.
paragraphs.cn: 这是重要的一天。我们要的是7月4日。我们不想要别的日子，因为这是《独立宣言》250周年。无与伦比。吉田（画外音）：特朗普为美国250周年举办的庆祝活动承诺了一场创纪录的烟花表演。

paragraphs.speaker: Stephen Vitale
paragraphs.text: We're going for the world record, but most importantly, we want the show to be memorable and esthetically beautiful for the crowd and something that people will remember forever. YOSHIDA (voice-over): Over the last two days, a fierce heat wave also forced organizers to alter or cancel events in the capital and in communities all along the East Coast. In Washington, D.C., the opening of the Great American State Fair was postponed by two hours, Saturday. Friday, it had to temporarily shut down due to the heat.
paragraphs.cn: 我们要冲击世界纪录，但最重要的是，我们希望表演令人难忘，对观众来说具有审美美感，成为人们永远铭记的事情。吉田（画外音）：过去两天，一场猛烈的热浪也迫使组织者改变或取消了首都和东海岸各社区的活动。在华盛顿特区，"美国大州博览会"的开幕时间周六推迟了两小时。周五，它曾因高温暂时关闭。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: We were told that they were closing it down due to the heat. A lot of people were passing out. YOSHIDA (voice-over): First responders were on hand to quickly respond to any emergencies.
paragraphs.cn: 我们被告知因高温关闭。很多人晕倒。吉田（画外音）：急救人员随时待命，迅速应对任何紧急情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Fourth of July is typically our busiest day of the year, where we run five to 600 calls a day. I think we're going to be over 1,000 this weekend. YOSHIDA (voice-over): The heat is also causing unhealthy air quality in some cities. On top of that, the extended fireworks displays will likely only make the air quality worse. In Washington, I'm Michael Yoshida, reporting. (END VIDEOTAPE)
paragraphs.cn: 7月4日通常是我们一年中最繁忙的一天，我们每天处理500到600个电话。我想这个周末会超过1000个。吉田（画外音）：高温还导致一些城市空气质量不健康。此外，长时间的烟花表演可能只会让空气质量更糟。在华盛顿，我是迈克尔·吉田报道。（结束录像）

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: All right. Thank you, Michael. We've also learned that more than 50 people were treated for heat related illnesses at the Freedom 250 celebration. And take a look at this. D.C. metro officials, they say that the heat caused this train to derail. Extreme heat can sometimes cause metal rails to buckle. No one was injured and all of the passengers were able to evacuate safely. Now in upstate New York, the heat is blamed for causing a bridge to get stuck in the up position.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。谢谢你，迈克尔。我们还了解到，在"自由250"庆祝活动中，有50多人因高温相关疾病接受了治疗。看看这个。华盛顿地铁官员表示，高温导致一列火车脱轨。极端高温有时会导致金属轨道弯曲。无人受伤，所有乘客均安全疏散。在纽约州北部，高温导致一座桥梁卡在升起位置。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: The heat caused the metal to expand to the point that the bridge could no longer close. Fireboats were brought in to hose it down to try to cool it off and get it working again. (BEGIN AUDIO CLIP) CAPT. MICHAEL KICK, BUFFALO FIRE DEPARTMENT (on the phone): We were out for about five hours, flowing water from the Buffalo River onto the Michigan Street Bridge in an attempt to cool it down. I estimate that we threw probably about 3 million gallons of water at the bridge while we were operating. (END AUDIO CLIP)
paragraphs.cn: 高温导致金属膨胀，使桥梁无法闭合。消防船被调来用水管喷水降温，试图使其恢复正常。（开始音频片段）布法罗消防局队长迈克尔·基克（电话中）：我们大约花了五个小时，从布法罗河抽水浇到密歇根街桥上，试图降温。我估计我们操作期间向桥上浇了大约300万加仑的水。（结束音频片段）

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: CNN meteorologist Allison Chinchar with us now to break down more of this. I mean, things finally starting to cool down a little bit, it seems, hopefully.
paragraphs.cn: CNN气象学家艾莉森·钦查尔现在加入我们，进一步分析情况。我的意思是，情况似乎终于开始有所缓解，希望如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Allison Chinchar
paragraphs.text: Yes. And even a couple places, believe it or not, may actually get below average in a few days from now. So you just got to get through really the kind of this last day of the heat in some areas for today, but vastly different than yesterday. Now, we do still have several ongoing showers and thunderstorms across much of the country. And you can see they're really scattered. We've got some just to the east of Chicago.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。而且，信不信由你，甚至有几个地方几天后可能会低于平均水平。所以，你只需要熬过今天部分地区这最后一天的炎热，但情况与昨天大不相同。目前，全国多地仍有阵雨和雷暴。你可以看到它们分布得很分散。芝加哥以东有一些。

paragraphs.speaker: Allison Chinchar
paragraphs.text: We've got other areas across the Central Plains, portions of the northeast that are also looking at some few areas of showers and thunderstorms. The main targets for today, it's going to be kind of a U-shaped here, where were looking at some of those showers and thunderstorms. It starts across portions of Texas and Oklahoma, stretches down through the southeast and then up into the mid-Atlantic and northeast. That's where you're going to have the best chance for those showers and thunderstorms.
paragraphs.cn: 其他地区，包括中央平原和东北部部分地区，也出现了一些阵雨和雷暴。今天的主要目标区域呈U形，那里将出现一些阵雨和雷暴。从德克萨斯州和俄克拉荷马州的部分地区开始，向东南延伸，然后进入大西洋中部和东北部。这些地区出现阵雨和雷暴的可能性最大。

paragraphs.speaker: Allison Chinchar
paragraphs.text: Damaging winds will be the primary concern. We could have some large hail, especially closer into the plains, and a lot of frequent lightning. So anybody that still has some outdoor activities left for today will have to contend with that. Here's a look at the forecast radar. Again, even as we go through the afternoon, you'll start to see more and more of them firing up across the Midwest, the southeast, southern plains, those kind of areas. They will continue through the evening and overnight timeline.
paragraphs.cn: 破坏性大风将是主要担忧。可能会出现大冰雹，尤其是在平原附近，以及频繁的闪电。因此，今天还有户外活动的人需要应对这些情况。以下是预报雷达图。同样，即使到了下午，你也会看到中西部、东南部、南部平原等地区出现越来越多的雷暴。它们将持续到傍晚和夜间。

paragraphs.speaker: Allison Chinchar
paragraphs.text: So again, these are going to be ongoing, really kind of those typical summertime pop up showers and thunderstorms. They fire up, they move out, you know, and then you get a little bit of a break afterwards. Speaking of break, we are finally going to get one from the heat. Take a look at this. Say Washington, D.C., for example. Keep in mind the average high this time of year is 89. We have been well above that the last few days. We will be pretty close to that starting tomorrow.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，这些将是持续的，典型的夏季突现阵雨和雷暴。它们出现，然后移走，之后你会得到一点喘息。说到喘息，我们终于要从高温中解脱出来了。看看这个。以华盛顿特区为例。记住，一年中这个时候的平均最高气温是89华氏度。过去几天我们一直远高于这个水平。从明天开始，我们将接近这个温度。

paragraphs.speaker: Allison Chinchar
paragraphs.text: And then by the time we get to the middle of the week, look at this 84. We're actually about five degrees below normal for a change. And that's going to be the case for areas of the mid-Atlantic, the northeast. But the opposite is going to take place in the Midwest. We're going to start to see those temperatures rebounding back up again after maybe about a day or two of a little bit of a mini break. But I think at this point, we'll take it.
paragraphs.cn: 然后到了周中，看这个84华氏度。我们实际上比正常水平低了大约5度。大西洋中部和东北部地区将出现这种情况。但中西部的情况正好相反。在经历了一两天的短暂休息后，气温将开始回升。但我认为，在这一点上，我们愿意接受。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: All right. The I-95 corridor needs to hear this today.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。I-95走廊今天需要听到这个消息。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Allison, thank you so much. Well, next up, a country in mourning. Iran's supreme leader is being laid to rest. We will explain what this means for negotiations with the U.S. moving forward. Plus, fireworks weren't the only thing lighting up the night sky over the Brooklyn Bridge. Details after the break. And we have some alarming cockpit audio. It was recorded when a Delta pilot reported that his plane was hit by fireworks. All that and more coming up.
paragraphs.cn: 艾莉森，非常感谢。接下来，一个哀悼中的国家。伊朗最高领袖正在下葬。我们将解释这对与美国谈判意味着什么。此外，烟花并不是布鲁克林大桥上空唯一照亮夜空的东西。详情请见稍后报道。我们还有一些令人震惊的驾驶舱录音。录音中，一名达美航空飞行员报告说他的飞机被烟花击中。所有这些以及更多内容即将呈现。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: This morning in the Middle East, crowds in Iran have been turning out for the second day of the public funeral for their slain supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Now Iran's health ministry says an estimated 15 million mourners are expected to take part in the public farewell. The supreme leader was killed by an Israeli strike on the first day of the joint U.S. Israeli war on Iran.
paragraphs.cn: 今天早上在中东，伊朗民众连续第二天参加被杀害的最高领袖阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊的公开葬礼。伊朗卫生部表示，预计将有约1500万哀悼者参加公开告别仪式。这位最高领袖是在美伊联合对伊朗战争的第一天被以色列空袭打死的。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: His sons were seen weeping at his funeral, but Khamenei's successor, his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, has so far not been seen nor heard from today. Let's go to CNN's Leila Gharagozlou with more. What have we seen? What have we watched play out as this nation is in mourning?
paragraphs.cn: 他的儿子们在葬礼上哭泣，但哈梅内伊的继任者、他的儿子莫杰塔巴·哈梅内伊至今未露面也未发声。让我们连线CNN的莱拉·加拉戈兹卢了解更多。我们看到了什么？在这个国家哀悼之际，我们目睹了什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Leila Gharagozlou
paragraphs.text: Yes. So today is the second day of the public mourning ceremony. So, average Iranians can go and pay their respects. We've seen quite an influx of people into the capital city, Tehran, to pay their respects. And this crowd is expected to get even bigger ahead of tomorrow's procession through the city. Now, we've seen a lot of outward displays of emotion and grief, which are very typical for Iranian funerals.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。今天是公开哀悼仪式的第二天。普通伊朗人可以前去表达敬意。我们看到大量人群涌入首都德黑兰表达敬意。预计在明天穿过城市的游行之前，人群会进一步扩大。我们看到许多外露的情感和悲伤，这在伊朗葬礼上非常典型。

paragraphs.speaker: Leila Gharagozlou
paragraphs.text: So you're seeing a lot of images of people crying, praying, prostrating themselves, including leaders like the speaker of parliament, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, and the president, Masoud Pezeshkian, just openly crying and showing their grief and emotion. I think what's really important to understand about this is that Ayatollah Ali Khamenei wasn't just a head of state. He is a really important figure within the Shiite Islamic world.
paragraphs.cn: 所以你看到很多人在哭泣、祈祷、跪拜，包括议会议长穆罕默德·巴吉尔·卡利巴夫和总统马苏德·佩泽什基安等领导人，他们公开哭泣，表达悲痛和情感。我认为需要理解的是，阿亚图拉阿里·哈梅内伊不仅仅是一位国家元首，他在什叶派伊斯兰世界中是一位非常重要的人物。

paragraphs.speaker: Leila Gharagozlou
paragraphs.text: So, his passing is one that is spreading beyond just the borders of Iran and is much more emotional than it might have been if it was just a world leader. Now, we're expecting to have, after the processions tomorrow, the funeral move to other key cities, Qom being one of them, which is one of the epicenters of Iran's religious life. A lot of the seminaries are there. From there, they'll move to Karbala and Najaf in Iraq before ending in Mashhad, which is the ayatollah's home town.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，他的逝世不仅限于伊朗境内，而且比仅仅是一位世界领导人去世时更加情绪化。现在，我们预计在明天的游行之后，葬礼将转移到其他主要城市，库姆是其中之一，它是伊朗宗教生活的中心之一。许多神学院都在那里。从那里，他们将前往伊拉克的卡尔巴拉和纳杰夫，最后到达哈梅内伊的家乡马什哈德。

paragraphs.speaker: Leila Gharagozlou
paragraphs.text: We don't know whether we're going to see his son Mojtaba Khamenei, as you mentioned. He has not been seen since he took over as supreme leader. If we do see him, it's likely that it could be on Thursday, but even that is very up in the air. So, a lot more funeral processions to come over the next six days, and everyone's going to be waiting to see if we see the new supreme leader.
paragraphs.cn: 我们不知道是否会看到他的儿子穆杰塔巴·哈梅内伊，正如你提到的。自从他接任最高领袖以来，他一直没有露面。如果我们看到他，很可能是在周四，但即使这一点也非常不确定。所以，未来六天还会有更多的葬礼游行，每个人都在等待是否能看到新的最高领袖。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: All right. A lot to keep tabs on over those next several days here. Leila Gharagozlou, thank you so much. Let's discuss this further with CNN military analyst and retired Air Force colonel Cedric Leighton. We also have CNN global affairs analyst Alex Plitsas joining us. You know, as we think about what Leila was just laying out this next few several days here, there's going to be a lot for the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。未来几天有很多事情需要关注。莱拉·加拉戈兹卢，非常感谢你。让我们与CNN军事分析师、退役空军上校塞德里克·莱顿进一步讨论。CNN全球事务分析师亚历克斯·普利萨斯也加入了我们。你知道，当我们思考莱拉刚刚描述的接下来几天的情况时，美国也有很多事情需要继续关注，因为有很多情绪可能会影响到需要重新启动或至少获得更稳固基础的谈判。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: to continue to watch as well as there are a lot of emotions that could play into talks that once again need to restart or at least get some more solid footing. Colonel Leighton, I wonder how much this and the talks to end the war are ongoing. How does this public funeral mark a new phase, if any, at all? COL. CEDRIC LEIGHTON (RET.),
paragraphs.cn: 莱顿上校，我想知道这在多大程度上以及正在进行的结束战争的谈判中，这场公开葬礼是否标志着新阶段的开始？

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Military Analyst
paragraphs.text: Well, Brad, good morning. I think what it does is, you know, first of all, it's a necessary passage for the Iranians to go through. You know, the funerals are extremely important for them, not only from a political standpoint, but also, as Leila pointed out, from a cultural and a religious perspective. So, this is going to be a critical moment for the Iranians.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，布拉德，早上好。我认为首先，这是伊朗人必须经历的一个必要过程。葬礼对他们来说极其重要，不仅从政治角度，而且正如莱拉指出的，从文化和宗教角度来看。所以，这对伊朗人来说将是一个关键时刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Military Analyst
paragraphs.text: And one of the key things to watch is the fact that many people in these funeral processions are calling for revenge, and that could very well color the Iranian response to any U.S. diplomatic overtures, any U.S. military movements, and, frankly, any U.S. political and diplomatic movements. So that's going to be, I think, a critical element here. And if that -- you know, if that colors the Iranian response, it could also signal a type of schism within the Iranians.
paragraphs.cn: 需要关注的关键点之一是，许多参加葬礼游行的人都在呼吁复仇，这很可能影响伊朗对美国任何外交姿态、任何军事行动，坦率地说，任何政治和外交行动的反应。所以，我认为这将是一个关键因素。如果这影响了伊朗的反应，也可能表明伊朗内部存在某种分裂。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Military Analyst
paragraphs.text: Some of the Iranian leadership is going to be in favor of some type of accommodation with the U.S., and others are going to be much more hard line. And a funeral like this gives the hardliners a lot more strength, basically, and increases their position and their leverage in a situation of this type.
paragraphs.cn: 一些伊朗领导层会倾向于与美国达成某种和解，而另一些则会更强硬。像这样的葬礼基本上给了强硬派更多的力量，并增加了他们在这种情况下的地位和影响力。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Alex, I wonder what should we read into and not read into as well on the late supreme leader's son and successor, Mojtaba Khamenei's absence at the funeral at least so far to this juncture?
paragraphs.cn: 亚历克斯，我想知道我们应该如何解读已故最高领袖的儿子和继任者穆杰塔巴·哈梅内伊至少在目前阶段缺席葬礼的情况，以及不应该过度解读什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Alex Plitsas
paragraphs.text: So, there are reports that have come out over the last several months that he was pretty severely wounded, potentially multiple surgeries on his arm. There could have been a prosthetic that was necessary for one of his legs, severe burns on his face. So, it's possible that this is due to the severe nature of the injuries that he sustained. There was some questions for a while as to whether or not he was actually alive. But by and large, and speaking to a number of the GCC partners in the gulf and then to U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 过去几个月有报道称他受了重伤，可能手臂做了多次手术。他的一条腿可能需要假肢，面部严重烧伤。所以，这可能是由于他受伤的严重性。有一段时间有人质疑他是否还活着。但总的来说，与海湾地区的海湾合作委员会伙伴以及美国方面交谈后……

paragraphs.speaker: Alex Plitsas
paragraphs.text: officials, the working theory now is that he is, in fact, alive and actively involved in state affairs, and this is likely due to his injuries.
paragraphs.cn: 官员们表示，目前的工作理论是，他实际上还活着，并且积极参与国家事务，这很可能是因为他的伤势。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: And so, Colonel Leighton, I wonder, as you were bringing up a moment ago, just to follow up on that, some of the mourners, they have expressed a desire for vengeance against the United States. What does this mean for U.S. national security threats, interests? How has all of that heightened as a result?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，莱顿上校，我想知道，正如您刚才提到的，就此跟进一下，一些哀悼者表达了报复美国的愿望。这对美国的国家安全威胁和利益意味着什么？这一切因此如何加剧了？

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.text: Well, certainly, I think increases at least -- potentially increases the threat level against U.S. forces especially in the Gulf region. But it also has implications worldwide. And Iran is very good at what we call asymmetric warfare. And what that means is, you know, it could be anything that is unconventional terrorist attacks that they sponsor through one of their proxies. Or it could mean cyber- attacks or a combination of those kinds of things.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，当然，我认为至少可能增加了对美国军队的威胁级别，尤其是在海湾地区。但这也对全球产生影响。伊朗非常擅长我们所说的非对称战争。这意味着，可能是他们通过某个代理人赞助的非传统恐怖袭击，也可能是网络攻击，或者这些手段的组合。

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.text: And one of the key elements, you know, of those terrorist attacks, especially, would be potentially, and I hate to bring this up, but targeted assassinations of key officials, and that is something that they are actively talking about, at least in public. Whether or not they actually do that, of course, is a very different issue.
paragraphs.cn: 而这些恐怖袭击的一个关键要素，尤其是，可能是 --  -- 我不想提这个 --  -- 针对关键官员的定点清除，这是他们正在积极讨论的，至少在公开场合。当然，他们是否真的这么做，则是另一个完全不同的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Alex, it is notable as well that NATO made sure to send a public congratulations to the U.S. on the 250th anniversary of its independence. I mean, as all of these different factors are playing out on the world stage and with diplomacy and with all of the posturing, do you expect that to make any difference in smoothing over bad feelings that Trump has towards NATO members here? And is the strategy to tread lightly?
paragraphs.cn: 亚历克斯，值得注意的是，北约在美国独立250周年之际特意向美国发出了公开祝贺。我的意思是，当所有这些不同因素在世界舞台上发挥作用，伴随着外交和种种姿态，您认为这能否有助于缓和特朗普对北约成员国的不满情绪？策略是轻描淡写吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Plitsas
paragraphs.text: I mean, it was appropriate for the alliance to send that message. And it was, I think, welcomed. But at the end of the day, the president has been aggravated with NATO partners and allies who he thought didn't come to his aid when he needed them during the war against Iran, even though it's been very clear for many of those allies that their own legal counsels have interpreted that there was no legal justification for their participation in strikes, let alone use of their bases in many cases.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，北约发出这一信息是合适的。我认为这受到了欢迎。但归根结底，总统对那些在他对伊朗战争期间需要帮助时没有伸出援手的北约伙伴和盟友感到恼火，尽管对许多盟友来说，他们的法律顾问已经明确表示，他们参与打击没有法律依据，更不用说在许多情况下使用他们的基地了。

paragraphs.speaker: Plitsas
paragraphs.text: So, that was part of the frustration. And then it's been since his first administration, the president's been pushing for NATO to take more responsibility, particularly the European allies for European security and seeing an increase in in spending on defense from three to five percent of GDP. So, he's still pushing those allies to spend more and take more responsibility for security. He's also, you know, been on the opposite side of the equation in many cases with Ukraine at least this administration.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，这是部分挫折感。而且自他第一个任期以来，总统一直推动北约承担更多责任，特别是欧洲盟友对欧洲安全的负责，并将国防开支从GDP的3%提高到5%。因此，他仍在推动这些盟友增加开支，为安全承担更多责任。此外，在许多情况下，他至少在本届政府中与乌克兰站在了对立面。

paragraphs.speaker: Plitsas
paragraphs.text: So, there's been a lot of tension. But the most recent issues that really has been -- drew his ire has been their lack of participation in the Iran strikes.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，一直存在很多紧张关系。但最近真正引起他愤怒的问题是他们没有参与对伊朗的打击。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Colonel, what is the reality that we would actually see NATO allies spending more? And what is the reality, the truth regarding that which Trump has said and railed about NATO allies not spending enough to support NATO?
paragraphs.cn: 上校，我们实际看到北约盟友增加开支的可能性有多大？特朗普所说并抱怨北约盟友没有为支持北约提供足够资金，这背后的现实和真相是什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.text: Yes. Well, really starting even before the Biden administration and the first Trump administration, NATO allies started to ramp up their defense spending. And it really reached a fever pitch, if you will, within the last decade or so. And in the Biden administration and also in this second Trump administration, NATO allies have actually increased their projected NATO defense spending.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。实际上，早在拜登政府和第一届特朗普政府之前，北约盟友就开始增加国防开支。可以说，在过去十年左右，这一趋势达到了高潮。在拜登政府和这第二届特朗普政府中，北约盟友实际上已经提高了他们计划的北约国防开支。

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.text: There are two countries in particular, Poland and Lithuania, that are either close to the five percent or have exceeded the five percent threshold of GDP spending. So, what the president is talking about is basically old news. What he really needs to focus on is the fact that the United States has convinced all these NATO countries to spend a lot more. And that includes countries like Germany, which historically been limited in their capacities and as well as in their willingness to spend more on defense.
paragraphs.cn: 有两个国家尤其突出，波兰和立陶宛，它们要么接近GDP的5%，要么已经超过了这个门槛。所以，总统所说的基本上是旧闻。他真正需要关注的是，美国已经说服了所有北约国家大幅增加开支。这包括德国这样的国家，历史上它们在能力和意愿上都有限，不愿在国防上投入更多。

paragraphs.speaker: Leighton
paragraphs.text: But by 2035, Germany will approach that threshold of five percent GDP. We have to keep in mind, the U.S. only spends at the moment, at least 2.8 percent of its GDP on defense. So, there's a big difference in what we're asking the NATO countries to do and what we're willing to do ourselves.
paragraphs.cn: 但到2035年，德国将接近GDP的5%这一门槛。我们必须记住，美国目前只将其GDP的至少2.8%用于国防。因此，我们要求北约国家做的事情与我们自己愿意做的事情之间存在巨大差异。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Alex, one of the ways that we've seen Trump in this 2.0 version of his presidency now, in the administration, really marked some of their wins, is how much spending other countries are willing to do into U.S. assets. For Turkey, for instance, there is a desire to buy F-35s from the U.S. How does that affect the NATO summit, especially in light of the adversarial relationship between Israel and Turkey?
paragraphs.cn: 亚历克斯，我们看到特朗普在2.0版总统任期内，政府真正标志其胜利的方式之一，就是其他国家愿意在美国资产上投入多少。例如，土耳其有意从美国购买F-35。这如何影响北约峰会，尤其是考虑到以色列和土耳其之间的敌对关系？

paragraphs.speaker: Plitsas
paragraphs.text: It's only gotten worse in recent years in terms of that adversarial relationship. I mean, if you speak to the Israelis, as I have over the last year or two, you really saw Iran as the long term strategic threat. And but now, due to some of the rhetoric that's been espoused by President Erdogan, if you speak to Israeli leaders, they see Turkey as a longer term strategic threat, which is obviously, you know, both odd and difficult as a NATO member and, you know, given tensions that have gone on.
paragraphs.cn: 近年来，这种敌对关系只会变得更糟。我的意思是，如果你和以色列人交谈，就像我过去一两年所做的那样，你真的会把伊朗视为长期战略威胁。但现在，由于埃尔多安总统发表的一些言论，如果你和以色列领导人交谈，他们认为土耳其是一个长期战略威胁，这显然既奇怪又困难，因为土耳其是北约成员国，而且考虑到持续的紧张局势。

paragraphs.speaker: Plitsas
paragraphs.text: The F-35 sale, as you know, Colonel Leighton has talked about many times, is really -- it had been controversial just given the fact that they hosted Russian air defense systems and other things. There was a significant, I think, tension about whether or not that should take place, you know, both within the State Department and the Pentagon to a certain extent. It looks like that may move forward.
paragraphs.cn: 正如你所知，莱顿上校多次谈到F-35的销售，这确实一直存在争议，仅仅因为他们部署了俄罗斯防空系统和其他因素。我认为，在国务院和五角大楼内部，对于是否应该进行这笔交易存在相当大的紧张情绪。现在看来，这可能会推进。

paragraphs.speaker: Plitsas
paragraphs.text: The president has developed a much better relationship with President Erdogan, particularly as he assisted with the transition in Syria and really making sure that from his perspective there was another semi-autonomous Kurdish state inside of Syria and that the Syrian Arab forces really took over. That's now the new government. So, he's really headed to the summit from what I was told by U.S. officials because President Erdogan asked him to.
paragraphs.cn: 总统与埃尔多安总统建立了更好的关系，特别是在他协助叙利亚过渡，并确保从他的角度来看，叙利亚境内没有另一个半自治的库尔德国家，而叙利亚阿拉伯军队真正接管了。现在这是新政府。所以，据美国官员告诉我，他前往峰会是因为埃尔多安总统邀请了他。

paragraphs.speaker: Plitsas
paragraphs.text: So, this was almost seen as a personal favor and a reflection of their relationship.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，这几乎被视为一种个人恩惠，反映了他们的关系。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Really valuable insights, both of you. Colonel Cedric Leighton and Alex Plitsas, thank you so much for joining us today.
paragraphs.cn: 两位都提供了非常有价值的见解。塞德里克·莱顿上校和亚历克斯·普利萨斯，非常感谢你们今天加入我们。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Well, next up, Paul Pelosi back in legal trouble. Why authorities say the former speaker's husband could face a hit and run charge. Stay with us.
paragraphs.cn: 接下来，保罗·佩洛西再次陷入法律麻烦。为什么当局说这位前议长的丈夫可能面临肇事逃逸指控。请继续关注。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Paul Pelosi could be facing a misdemeanor charge for a hit and run accident in California's wine country. Police say the 86-year-old husband of former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi drove his convertible into a legally parked car on Friday, stopped briefly and then drove off. He was found about a quarter of a mile away telling officers he wasn't sure what happened. He was not arrested and no one was hurt. Authorities say that Pelosi did not have alcohol in his system.
paragraphs.cn: 保罗·佩洛西可能因加州葡萄酒之乡的一起肇事逃逸事故面临轻罪指控。警方称，这位86岁的前众议院议长南希·佩洛西的丈夫周五驾驶他的敞篷车撞上了一辆合法停放的汽车，短暂停车后驶离。他在大约四分之一英里外被发现，告诉警官他不确定发生了什么。他没有被逮捕，也没有人受伤。当局表示，佩洛西体内没有酒精。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Firefighters in New York had to put out several small fires last night on the Brooklyn Bridge. The flames broke out at the end of the Macy's fireworks show. No one was injured and it's not clear what sparked the fires and police are not saying whether fireworks may have caused them. And a frightening moment in the skies over Chicago during the Fourth of July celebrations. A Delta flight from Atlanta appears to have been struck by fireworks while landing at Midway Airport.
paragraphs.cn: 昨晚，纽约消防员不得不扑灭布鲁克林大桥上的几处小火。火势在梅西百货烟花表演结束时燃起。无人受伤，起火原因尚不清楚，警方也未说明烟花是否导致火灾。此外，在芝加哥的国庆庆祝活动中，空中出现惊险一幕：一架从亚特兰大起飞的达美航空航班在米德韦机场降落时，似乎被烟花击中。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Listen to what the pilots told air traffic control. (BEGIN AUDIO CLIP)
paragraphs.cn: 听听飞行员对空中交通管制说了什么。（音频开始）

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: We're just hoping it was just the mortar that went off underneath but definitely felt the big bang.
paragraphs.cn: 我们只希望是下面的迫击炮爆炸了，但确实感觉到了巨大的冲击。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: There have been multiple reports as you can imagine. The city's aware. They said they would notify the Chicago police, but you know, I don't know what they'll do.
paragraphs.cn: 你可以想象，有多份报告。市政府知情。他们说会通知芝加哥警方，但你知道，我不知道他们会怎么做。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Southwest 223, just for your information the preceding Airbus said they were struck by a firework approximately 200 AGL. (END AUDIO CLIP)
paragraphs.cn: 西南航空223，仅供参考，前面的空客飞机说他们在大约200英尺高度被烟花击中。（音频结束）

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Delta says the aircraft landed safely and is now being inspected. Authorities are looking into how fireworks may have gotten that close to planes approaching the airport. Well, the President's promised Fourth of July rally speech took on a different tone than expected. It mostly stuck to scripts thanking veterans highlighting historic U.S. flags and wrapped it up by promising to take the country to new heights.
paragraphs.cn: 达美航空表示，飞机已安全降落，目前正在接受检查。当局正在调查烟花如何能如此接近接近机场的飞机。总统承诺的国庆集会演讲基调与预期不同。演讲基本照本宣科，感谢退伍军人，强调美国历史旗帜，并以承诺将国家带向新高度结束。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: This is only the dawn of the golden age of America and on this 250th Fourth of July we declare just as they did two and a half centuries ago that for our country and for our children and for the cause of liberty, we are going to take our country to new levels to levels not reached. We're going to make it bigger better stronger and we're going to love it even more.
paragraphs.cn: 这只是美国黄金时代的黎明，在这个第250个国庆日，我们像两个半世纪前那样宣告，为了我们的国家、我们的孩子和自由的事业，我们将把国家带到前所未有的新高度。我们将让它更大、更好、更强，我们将更加热爱它。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: The President also used the moment to slam communism and tout his controversial Save America Act to overall federal elections. Joining me now, we've got Spectrum News political anchor Errol Louis. He's also the host of The Big Deal with Errol Louis. Errol good to have you here with us today. Let's just talk about the overall message here. He built this as a Trump rally but it was somewhat of a departure of his typical rally style speeches.
paragraphs.cn: 总统还借此机会抨击共产主义，并推销其有争议的《拯救美国法案》以全面改革联邦选举。现在加入我们的是Spectrum News政治主播Errol Louis，他也是《Errol Louis的大交易》的主持人。Errol，很高兴你今天能来。我们来谈谈整体信息。他把这次演讲打造成特朗普集会，但与他典型的集会风格演讲有所不同。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: What was your reading even if he did get into political topics? The main takeaway that anyone who was watching or listening or attending should have gotten from that speech.
paragraphs.cn: 即使他涉及政治话题，你的解读是什么？任何观看、收听或参加的人应该从那次演讲中得到的主要信息是什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Errol Louis
paragraphs.text: Good morning, Brad. It was a really a repeat of what he'd done the night before in front of Mount Rushmore and it combined you know 80 to 90 percent what you would expect on an occasion like this. Some words that hopefully will be echoing down through the ages trying to sort of meet the moment and rise to the occasion for something as momentous as the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence.
paragraphs.cn: 早上好，Brad。这实际上是他前一天晚上在拉什莫尔山前所做演讲的重复，结合了80%到90%你在这种场合会期待的内容。一些话语希望能世代传颂，试图在《独立宣言》250周年这样重大的时刻，顺应时势，不负众望。

paragraphs.speaker: Errol Louis
paragraphs.text: On the other hand, being Donald Trump he could not resist a chance to do a little bit of politics and a little bit of legislating and trying to sort of push for the legislation that he wants passed. And when he says he wants that Save America Act passed he combined it with a prediction that Republicans would win for the next hundred years if they got it passed. And that takes us right into the realm of a very controversial piece of legislation that by the way is not likely to pass.
paragraphs.cn: 另一方面，作为唐纳德·特朗普，他无法抗拒机会来搞点政治、搞点立法，试图推动他想要通过的法案。当他说他希望《拯救美国法案》通过时，他还预测说，如果该法案通过，共和党将在未来一百年赢得胜利。这让我们进入了一个非常有争议的立法领域，顺便说一句，该法案不太可能通过。

paragraphs.speaker: Errol Louis
paragraphs.text: I mean this was the President putting out his legislative wish list in the middle of what would have been a much bigger broader ceremony for most of us watching.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，这是总统在本来对我们大多数人来说会更宏大、更广泛的仪式中提出他的立法愿望清单。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Well, I mean well it's a day that ends in why. So of course, the Save America Act was going to be brought up by President Trump and he did that made the arguments. We should also note in the doubling down on that bill refusing to sign off on bipartisan legislation like the housing affordability overhaul bill as well until the Save America Act is passed. But there's not wide ranging support.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我的意思是，这一天以"为什么"结束。所以当然，特朗普总统会提出《拯救美国法案》，他确实这样做了并提出了论点。我们还应该注意到，他在该法案上加倍下注，拒绝签署两党立法，比如住房可负担性改革法案，直到《拯救美国法案》通过。但并没有广泛的支持。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: In fact, the House Speaker had to make sure that he was even amid some of the Republican infighting making sure that he was letting people go home early for vacation because there was so much disdain. And so, what is the reality moving forward. What does this mean for other legislation as well that can actually meaningful -- meaningful -- meaningfully help American households.
paragraphs.cn: 事实上，众议院议长不得不确保他甚至在共和党内部争斗中，确保他让人们提前回家度假，因为存在如此多的不满。那么，未来的现实是什么？这对其他能够真正帮助美国家庭的立法意味着什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.text: Well, it says something about Congress that as dysfunctional as it's been as troubled as it has been in this session they could get the housing bill passed. I mean that that there was in fact some support for it.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，这说明了国会的一些情况：尽管本届会议功能失调、问题重重，但他们还是能够通过住房法案。我的意思是，实际上确实有一些支持。

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.text: The problem of course is that the Save America Act is the opposite of bipartisan legislation that it would require the kind of identification to vote that something an estimated 21 million Americans do not have, including 9 million women who may have changed their names after marriage or for other reasons. It's really just not a workable piece of legislation. And, you know, the White House has just refused to accept that. Donald Trump has refused to accept that.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，问题在于《拯救美国法案》与两党立法相反，它要求投票时提供某种身份证明，而估计有2100万美国人没有这种证明，其中包括900万可能因结婚或其他原因改名的女性。这确实是一项不可行的立法。而且，你知道，白宫一直拒绝接受这一点。唐纳德·特朗普拒绝接受这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.text: But members of Congress, even big supporters of the President have said we just can't do this. We do not want to throw our elections into chaos. That starts to affect all of the members of Congress, by the way. So even if they support the President, they don't necessarily want their own jobs to be at risk or thrown into the middle of a lot of confusion in the fall.
paragraphs.cn: 但国会议员，甚至总统的大力支持者都说，我们不能这样做。我们不想让我们的选举陷入混乱。顺便说一句，这开始影响所有国会议员。所以即使他们支持总统，他们也不一定希望自己的工作面临风险，或者在秋天陷入混乱之中。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: You know, while we have you, Trump also took the opportunity to slam communism. I mean, we were talking in the break just about like who is praising communism right now. Trump continues to bring this up, an effort to kind of muddle that term with democratic socialism, which has seen some pretty marked wins going into the midterm elections in the Democratic primaries as well. I want to play a quick clip of that for our viewers and then we'll get your reaction on the other side.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，趁你还在，特朗普还借机抨击共产主义。我的意思是，我们在休息时谈到谁现在在赞扬共产主义。特朗普继续提起这一点，试图将这个词与民主社会主义混为一谈，而民主社会主义在中期选举和民主党初选中都取得了一些显著胜利。我想为我们的观众播放一段简短的片段，然后我们听听你的反应。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.text: America will never be a communist country. Won't happen. Communism is a loser and it always will be. The communist system is the opposite of the American system and the communist system has never worked. Our warriors did not fight communism on battlefields across the world only to have that menace rear its ugly head right back here in America. We're not going to let it happen.
paragraphs.cn: 美国永远不会成为共产主义国家。不会发生。共产主义是失败者，而且永远都是。共产主义制度与美国制度相反，共产主义制度从未奏效。我们的战士没有在世界各地的战场上与共产主义作战，却让这种威胁在美国本土重新抬头。我们不会让它发生。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: OK, so, Errol, I think I was in seventh grade social studies in Montgomery Middle School in New Jersey when we learned that communism is not socialism. What is Trump getting at here?
paragraphs.cn: 好的，那么，埃罗尔，我想我在新泽西州蒙哥马利中学七年级社会研究课上就学到共产主义不是社会主义。特朗普到底想说什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.text: Well, he's suggesting, I think, Brad, that what the Republicans should run on is an all-out attack on the Democratic opponents that they have by calling them all communists. Now, that's not a very viable strategy, you know, because the polling clearly indicates, as well as, frankly, the results of a lot of recent elections, that people want paid sick leave. They want health care that they can afford. They want affordable groceries at the store.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，布拉德，我认为他是在暗示，共和党应该全力攻击民主党对手，给他们贴上共产主义者的标签。但这不是一个可行的策略，因为民调清楚地显示，而且坦率地说，最近许多选举的结果也表明，人们想要带薪病假。他们想要负担得起的医疗保健。他们想要商店里价格合理的食品杂货。

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.text: And anybody who's trying to deliver that, which is kind of what Democrats are running on this season, that they're going to really be amenable to it. And you can try and brand it as communism or anything else. But the reality is it's just not likely to work. So this is Donald Trump making arguments from a prior generation. You know, I'm old enough to remember the Soviet Union. And you would call anybody who was like left leaning.
paragraphs.cn: 任何试图提供这些的人 --  -- 这基本上就是民主党这个选举季的主张 --  -- 都会受到欢迎。你可以试图把它贴上共产主义或其他任何标签。但现实是，这很可能行不通。所以这是唐纳德·特朗普在用上一代人的论点。你知道，我年纪大到还记得苏联。那时你会把任何左倾的人称为……

paragraphs.speaker: Louis
paragraphs.text: You could say, well, you're a fellow traveler or, you know, there'd be all these kind of insinuations and accusations. And it could move votes in some elections. But I don't think that's going to happen this year.
paragraphs.cn: 你可以说，嗯，你是个同路人，或者，你知道，会有各种这样的暗示和指控。这在某些选举中可能会影响选票。但我认为今年不会发生这种情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: All right. Old enough to remember, but still a lot of youth on you. Errol, thanks so much for taking the time.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。年纪大到还记得，但你仍然充满活力。埃罗尔，非常感谢你抽出时间。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Well, next up, control of Congress is up for grabs. Razor thin margins. Key states in play. How the fight for Congress is shaping up to be one of the toughest midterm battles ever. And if you're heading out, remember, you can stream our show from anywhere in the U.S. right from the CNN app. You can also go to CNN.com/Watch.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，接下来，国会的控制权悬而未决。优势微乎其微。关键州处于争夺中。国会之争如何成为有史以来最激烈的中期选举之一。如果你要出门，请记住，你可以通过CNN应用在美国任何地方观看我们的节目。你也可以访问CNN.com/Watch。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: A live look at a quiet Capitol Hill this morning. This November will mark one of the hardest-fought midterm elections in U.S. history. The GOP has a razor-thin margin in the House and an advantage that is narrowing in the Senate. It's where data shows a number of states could flip. Here's CNN's Arlette Saenz with more. (BEGIN VIDEOTAPE)
paragraphs.cn: 今早国会山一片宁静。今年11月将标志着美国历史上竞争最激烈的中期选举之一。共和党在众议院拥有微弱优势，在参议院的优势正在缩小。数据显示，多个州可能易主。以下是CNN的Arlette Saenz带来的详细报道。（开始录像）

paragraphs.speaker: Arlette Saenz
paragraphs.text: The battle for control of the U.S. Senate is set to play out over nine states between now and November. And for the first time, CNN is ranking the list of states most likely to flip in this midterm election. Now, Democrats need to flip four Republican-held seats and defend all of their own if they want to win the majority in November. And topping the list that we have is North Carolina, which is currently held by a Republican senator who is retiring.
paragraphs.cn: 从现在到11月，美国参议院控制权之争将在九个州展开。CNN首次对本次中期选举中最可能易主的州进行了排名。民主党如果想在11月赢得多数席位，需要翻转四个共和党控制的席位，并守住自己所有的席位。排名第一的是北卡罗来纳州，该席位目前由一位即将退休的共和党参议员占据。

paragraphs.speaker: Arlette Saenz
paragraphs.text: But the Democratic candidate, former Governor Roy Cooper, is giving the Democratic Party one of their best chances at flipping a Senate seat in November. He is facing off against Republican candidate Michael Whatley, a close ally of President Donald Trump. And if you take a look at polling, Cooper has -- is currently leading Whatley by seven points in a state where a Democratic senator has not won since 2008.
paragraphs.cn: 但民主党候选人、前州长罗伊·库珀为民主党提供了11月翻转参议院席位的最佳机会之一。他的对手是共和党候选人迈克尔·沃特利，他是唐纳德·特朗普总统的亲密盟友。从民调来看，库珀目前领先沃特利7个百分点，而该州自2008年以来从未有民主党参议员获胜。

paragraphs.speaker: Arlette Saenz
paragraphs.text: Now, coming in at number two, we move up north to Maine, a seat currently held by Republican Senator Susan Collins, who is running for her sixth term in office. She is facing off against the progressive Democratic candidate Graham Platner in a race where his campaign has been embroiled in a lot of controversies in recent months. But if you take a look at polling, this race is very competitive, and there is no clear leader in the contest at this point in the race.
paragraphs.cn: 排名第二的是缅因州，该席位目前由共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯占据，她正在寻求第六个任期。她的对手是进步派民主党候选人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳，他的竞选活动近几个月来陷入诸多争议。但从民调来看，这场竞选竞争非常激烈，目前没有明显的领先者。

paragraphs.speaker: Arlette Saenz
paragraphs.text: So that will be one to certainly watch heading into November. Coming in at number three is the state of Michigan, which is currently held by a Democrat, retiring Senator Gary Peters. And the Democratic primary has been very competitive and contentious between three candidates. That includes the progressive candidate Abdul El-Sayed, the moderate Congresswoman Haley Stevens and state Senator Mallory McMorrow.
paragraphs.cn: 所以这将是11月值得关注的一场竞选。排名第三的是密歇根州，该席位目前由民主党人、即将退休的参议员加里·彼得斯占据。民主党初选在三位候选人之间竞争非常激烈且充满争议。这包括进步派候选人阿卜杜勒·埃尔-赛义德、温和派众议员海莉·史蒂文斯和州参议员马洛里·麦克莫罗。

paragraphs.speaker: Arlette Saenz
paragraphs.text: This primary really captures a lot of the debates playing out within the Democratic Party at this moment in time, as we have seen in recent elections success from some progressive candidates. The question is whether that can also translate to a presidential battleground state. Whoever wins that primary on August 4th will face off against the Republican former Congressman Mike Rogers. Now, coming in at four, five, and six are three states that are in deep red territory. There is Ohio, then Alaska, and then Iowa.
paragraphs.cn: 这场初选确实捕捉到了当前民主党内部许多辩论的焦点，正如我们在近期选举中看到一些进步派候选人取得成功一样。问题在于，这种势头能否也转化为总统大选关键州的胜利。无论谁在8月4日的初选中胜出，都将面对共和党前众议员迈克·罗杰斯。现在，排名第四、第五和第六的是三个深红州：俄亥俄州、阿拉斯加州和爱荷华州。

paragraphs.speaker: Arlette Saenz
paragraphs.text: Iowa, if you take a look at recent polling, there's no clear leader in that contest between Congresswoman Ashley Hinson and the Democratic candidate Josh Turek. The President's economic policies, like tariffs and the fallout from the war with Iran, is something that could play heavily in that contest. Now, coming in at number seven is the state of Georgia.
paragraphs.cn: 爱荷华州，如果你看看最近的民调，众议员阿什利·辛森与民主党候选人乔什·图雷克之间的竞争没有明显的领先者。总统的经济政策，比如关税以及与伊朗战争的后果，可能会在这场竞争中发挥重要作用。现在，排名第七的是佐治亚州。

paragraphs.speaker: Arlette Saenz
paragraphs.text: This is a battleground state that is currently held by Senator Jon Ossoff, the only Democratic senator running in a state where President Donald Trump won back in 2024. But Ossoff has proven to be a very strong fundraiser. He has this ability to have clips of his speeches go viral. And he's giving a lot of Democrats hope that they will be able to hold on to this state.
paragraphs.cn: 这是一个关键战场州，目前由参议员乔恩·奥索夫把持，他是唯一一位在唐纳德·特朗普总统于2024年赢得的州中竞选的民主党参议员。但奥索夫已被证明是一位非常强大的筹款人。他有一种能力，能让他的演讲片段在网上疯传。他给了许多民主党人希望，认为他们能够守住这个州。

paragraphs.speaker: Arlette Saenz
paragraphs.text: If you take a look at recent polling, he was up over his opponent, GOP opponent, Congressman Mike Collins, by 13 points, fueling some of the Democratic hopes in that state. Coming in at number eight is the state of New Hampshire, which is currently in Democratic control. And at the bottom of our list, at number nine, is the state of Texas.
paragraphs.cn: 如果你看看最近的民调，他领先对手 --  -- 共和党对手众议员迈克·柯林斯 --  -- 13个百分点，这助长了民主党在该州的希望。排名第八的是新罕布什尔州，目前由民主党控制。在我们名单的末尾，第九名是得克萨斯州。

paragraphs.speaker: Arlette Saenz
paragraphs.text: This is a contest that's going to dominate a lot of the conversation heading into November, as Democrats do see some hope in James Talarico, who is facing off against Republican State Attorney General Ken Paxton, someone who has endured a lot of scandals in his state, but he has also survived those in his past political campaigns. There's a lot of questions about whether Texas will actually come into play. And we will keep an eye on that as this race progresses.
paragraphs.cn: 这场竞争将主导进入11月前的许多讨论，因为民主党人确实在詹姆斯·塔拉里科身上看到了一些希望，他将面对共和党州总检察长肯·帕克斯顿，后者在该州经历了许多丑闻，但在过去的政治竞选中也幸存了下来。关于得克萨斯州是否真的会成为竞争州，存在很多疑问。我们将随着这场竞选的进展而密切关注。

paragraphs.speaker: Arlette Saenz
paragraphs.text: But right now, this list really serves as a snapshot of this moment in time, of where these races currently stand. And it could shift between now and November, as there are still four months to go in a very contentious and spirited fight for control of the U.S. Senate. Arlette Saenz, CNN, Washington. (END VIDEOTAPE)
paragraphs.cn: 但现在，这份名单确实反映了当前这些竞选所处的状况。从现在到11月，情况可能会发生变化，因为距离一场争夺美国参议院控制权的激烈而充满活力的斗争还有四个月。阿尔莱特·萨恩斯，CNN，华盛顿报道。（录像结束）

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Thank you, Arlette. On America's Independence Day, the first American pope delivered a message far beyond the Vatican by his visit to a migrant gateway is drawing global attention.
paragraphs.cn: 谢谢你，阿尔莱特。在美国独立日，第一位美国教皇通过访问移民门户传达了一个远超梵蒂冈的信息，这引起了全球关注。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Let's get a check of your top stories. Extreme heat and severe storms delayed but did not stop Fourth of July celebrations around the country. The nation's capital. You saw crowds there pack the National Mall to watch what was billed as the largest fireworks display in history. President Trump spoke at the event touting American strength and warning against the threat of communism. And new this morning, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu plans to visit the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 让我们看看你的头条新闻。极端高温和严重风暴虽然推迟了，但并未阻止全国各地的七月四日庆祝活动。在首都，你看到人群挤满了国家广场，观看号称史上最大规模的烟花表演。特朗普总统在活动中发表讲话，宣扬美国的力量，并警告共产主义的威胁。今晨最新消息：以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡计划最早于下周一访问美国。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: to meet with President Trump as soon as next Monday. That is according to an Israeli source. The two leaders have not met in person since the start of the war with Iran. And Tyler Robinson, the man accused of fatally shooting conservative activist Charlie Kirk, will be in court this week. Prosecutors are expected to use the multi-day hearing to preview some of their evidence against Robinson. Kirk's wife and family are expected to attend.
paragraphs.cn: 与特朗普总统会面。这是根据一位以色列消息人士的说法。自与伊朗的战争开始以来，两位领导人尚未亲自会面。被指控枪杀保守派活动家查理·柯克的泰勒·罗宾逊本周将出庭。检察官预计将利用为期多天的听证会预览他们对罗宾逊的部分证据。柯克的妻子和家人预计将出席。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: This morning, as Pope Leo leads prayers at the Vatican, his July 4th message is still resonating. The first American pope spent the holiday on the Italian island of Lampedusa, one of the main gateways for migrants crossing into Europe. Thousands arrive there each year, and many are lost at sea. The Pope honored those who died trying to make the crossing, visiting a migrant cemetery on the island. For more on the significance of this visit, we bring in CNN's Christopher Lamb in Italy.
paragraphs.cn: 今晨，当教皇利奥在梵蒂冈领导祈祷时，他的七月四日信息仍在回响。这位第一位美国教皇在意大利兰佩杜萨岛度过了这个节日，该岛是移民进入欧洲的主要门户之一。每年有数千人抵达那里，许多人在海上失踪。教皇向那些试图穿越而遇难的人致敬，参观了岛上的一个移民墓地。关于这次访问的更多意义，我们请来CNN的克里斯托弗·兰姆在意大利报道。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: Extremely significant from the first American pope here, Christopher. CHRISTOPHER LAMB, CNN VATICAN CORRESPONDENT (on-camera): Yes, a very strong message from Pope Leo during this July 4th independence weekend. The Pope emphasizing the importance of protecting and welcoming migrants, not just in Europe, but also in the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 来自首位美国教皇的消息极为重要，克里斯托弗。CNN梵蒂冈记者克里斯托弗·兰姆（出镜）：是的，教皇利奥在七月四日独立日周末发出了非常强烈的信息。教皇强调保护和欢迎移民的重要性，不仅在欧洲，也在美国。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith
paragraphs.text: The Pope from Lampedusa sending a message to Americans, calling on them to welcome immigrants with compassion and generosity, pointing to the history the United States has of welcoming migrants. Now, whilst in Lampedusa, he really sought to highlight the tragic human face of the migrants crisis, going to a cemetery where there was a grave for a six-month-old who died in a shipwreck in 2020, and highlighting the need to welcome and protect migrants in a comprehensive manner in Europe.
paragraphs.cn: 教皇从兰佩杜萨向美国人发出信息，呼吁他们以同情和慷慨的态度欢迎移民，并指出美国有欢迎移民的历史。在兰佩杜萨期间，他确实试图突出移民危机中悲惨的人道主义面孔，前往一个墓地，那里有一个2020年海难中死亡的六个月大婴儿的坟墓，并强调需要以全面的方式在欧洲欢迎和保护移民。

paragraphs.speaker: Smith

Brad.
paragraphs.cn: 教皇确实抽时间庆祝了七月四日。回到罗马后，他去了美国驻圣座大使的官邸，与大使的家人共度时光，据称他得到了自制的苹果派、一件美国足球队T恤和一个棒球。布拉德。

paragraphs.cn: 在华盛顿，经过近两个小时的暴风雨延误后，特朗普总统登上中心舞台，为当天的活动画上句号。总统的演讲大部分致力于美国的历史和实力。他宣扬了过去的美国军事胜利，并加入了他政府发起的一些军事行动。唐纳德·特朗普（共和党人）：

paragraphs.cn: 特朗普还感谢了为美国自由而战的英雄和退伍军人。他请出了三位二战退伍军人，称他们为真正的"最伟大的一代"。虽然总统基本照本宣科，但他的讲话中确实带有政治色彩。他在演讲中多次提到共产主义，并主张通过《拯救美国法案》，这是他提出的旨在彻底改革联邦选举的法案。CNN的朱莉娅·本布鲁克带来更多关于特朗普总统演讲的报道。

paragraphs.speaker: Julia Benbrook

Weather-related concerns led to evacuations on the National Mall and delays, but Trump made it clear that he planned to speak no matter what. Writing on social media, "Storms bring luck to whatever the occasion. They also make events a little bit more exciting. We will wait it out. I don't care if it's 2 o'clock in the morning or in one hour from now," he said.
paragraphs.cn: 在华盛顿，漫长的一天过后，唐纳德·特朗普总统终于在东部时间晚上11点后不久登台演讲。与天气相关的担忧导致国家广场疏散和延误，但特朗普明确表示，无论如何他都要演讲。他在社交媒体上写道："风暴给任何场合都带来好运。它们也让活动更刺激。我们会等它过去。我不在乎是凌晨2点还是从现在起一小时后。"

paragraphs.speaker: Julia Benbrook
paragraphs.text: Adding, "Looks like it's going to pass. They always do." A message that he reiterated when he spoke to the crowd and honored America's 250th birthday.
paragraphs.cn: 他补充说："看起来它会过去。它们总是会过去。"他在向人群讲话并纪念美国250岁生日时重申了这一信息。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump

paragraphs.cn: 可以想象，有多起报告。市政府知情。他们说会通知芝加哥警方，但，你知道，我不知道他们会怎么做。西南航空223，仅供参考，前面的空客说他们被烟花击中，大约200
